zotero.abstractNote: Background: Brain metastases (BM) are a frequent and severe complication in patients with non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC). Early identification of patients at high risk for BM development could improve clinical management. We aimed to develop a machine-learning (ML) model to predict the development of BM in patients with NSCLC. Patients and Methods: We retrospectively analyzed 248 patients with NSCLC treated at our institution between 2010 and 2019. Clinical, radiological, and histopathological data were collected. We developed an ML model using a random forest classifier to predict the development of BM. The model was trained and validated using a 5-fold cross-validation approach. Results: The final model achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) of 0.78 (95% CI, 0.71-0.85) for predicting BM development. The most important predictors were the presence of lymph node metastases, tumor size, and the histological subtype. Conclusion: Our ML model shows promising performance in predicting the development of BM in patients with NSCLC. This tool could potentially assist clinicians in identifying high-risk patients who might benefit from closer surveillance or prophylactic interventions.
